title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Northeastern University's President Joseph Aoun reported a total compensation of $3,045,428 for Fiscal Year 2025.
Aoun's compensation increase was largely due to $700,000 in deferred compensation and $550,000 in bonuses.
Aoun's earnings surpassed MIT President Sally Kornbluth by nearly $1 million, making him the highest-paid in Massachusetts.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge.
The shooting occurred shortly before 4:30 a.m.; Bautista's body was discovered by a passerby around 5:30 a.m.
Cambridge police and Massachusetts State Police detectives are investigating; the public can report tips anonymously.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major, a New England Aquarium trainer, volunteered in South Africa to aid endangered penguin chicks.
Major spent two weeks caring for about 100 penguin chicks as part of an international conservation effort.
African penguins have lost 97% of their population, facing extinction by 2035 without continued conservation efforts.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras apologized after a benches-clearing brawl with the Nationals, for which he's appealing a seven-game suspension.
Contreras faces emotional challenges following two earthquakes in Venezuela, impacting his on-field behavior and resulting in multiple ejections.
Contreras and Nationals' Cade Cavalli, both suspended, have exchanged apologies and expressed hope for mutual respect moving forward.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez have been selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman has a 2.10 ERA with 33 strikeouts and 17 saves in 25.2 innings this season.
Suarez, with a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts, returns to play in Philadelphia, his former home team.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce expressed disappointment but not surprise at the Celtics trading Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Pierce encouraged Brown, noting past perceived slights should fuel his drive and career advancement.
Jaylen Brown stated Boston's decision to trade him showed a lack of respect from the organization.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston commemorated the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence with a parade and reading at the Old State House.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular included performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper, accompanied by a drone show.
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu attended the Independence Day celebrations, which attracted large crowds to the city's historic sites and Esplanade.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads Red Sox in OPS (.977) and home runs (7) over the last 30 days.
Durbin improved from a .163 average in early 2026 to a .321 average since May 28.
Durbin's contract allows Red Sox team control until 2031, aiding future salary cap flexibility.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman set a new MLB record for most strikeouts by a relief pitcher, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm with 1,364 strikeouts.
Chapman recorded the milestone strikeout against the Los Angeles Angels, helping secure a 5-2 victory for the Boston Red Sox.
Despite Chapman's achievement, he might be traded by the August deadline, as the Red Sox hold a 13% playoff chance.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranked as the most collision-prone city in the U.S. for the second consecutive year, per Allstate's report.
Worcester and Springfield, Massachusetts, ranked near the bottom with average collision spans of 5.14 and 5.18 years, respectively.
Providence, Rhode Island, also featured among the 10 riskiest cities, with drivers averaging 5.87 years between collisions.
